title: The Weather Forecast
learningGoal: The learner will accurately extract predictive sequences from a weather report, successfully decoding highly compressed, native-speed clitic contractions (It'll, I'll, We'll).
instructions: Listen to a radio weather report and a man talking to his wife about their weekend plans. They speak very fast! Listen closely for the squished words: 'It'll', 'I'll', and 'We'll'. Answer the 8 questions.

contentItems.text: When the weatherman talks about tomorrow, he doesn't say 'It will rain'. He says 'It'll rain' to save time.

listeningGoal: Parse rapid phonological contractions of the 'will' modal to accurately determine future events.
audioScenario: A couple listening to the local weather forecast to decide what to do tomorrow.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Good evening! Here is the weather for tomorrow.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: गुड इवनिंग! हियर इज़ द वेदर फॉर टुमॉरो।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: शुभ संध्या! पेश है कल का मौसम।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: In the morning, it'll be very sunny and hot.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: इन द मॉर्निंग, इट'अल बी वेरी सनी एंड हॉट।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: सुबह के समय बहुत धूप और गर्मी रहेगी।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: But in the afternoon, it'll rain heavily. It will be cold.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: बट इन দি आफ्टरन्यून, इट'अल रेन हेविली। इट विल बी कोल्ड।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: लेकिन दोपहर में, भारी बारिश होगी। ठंड होगी।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Did you hear that, Amit? It'll rain tomorrow afternoon.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: डिड यू हियर दैट, अमित? इट'अल रेन टुमॉरो आफ्टरन्यून।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: क्या तुमने सुना, अमित? कल दोपहर बारिश होगी।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes. That is bad news. We'll have to change our plans.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस। दैट इज़ बैड न्यूज़। वी'ल हैव टू चेंज आवर प्लांस।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ। वह बुरी खबर है। हमें अपनी योजनाएं बदलनी होंगी।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: What will we do? We can't go to the park.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: वॉट विल वी डू? वी कांट गो टू द पार्क।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हम क्या करेंगे? हम पार्क नहीं जा सकते।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: I know. In the morning, I'll wash the car because it'll be sunny.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आई नो। इन द मॉर्निंग, आई'ल वॉश द कार बिकॉज़ इट'अल बी सनी।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मुझे पता है। मैं सुबह अपनी कार धोऊँगा क्योंकि धूप होगी।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay. And what about the afternoon?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके। एंड वॉट अबाऊट दि आफ्टरन्यून?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है। और दोपहर के बारे में क्या?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: I think we'll stay inside. I'll read my book.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आई थिंक वी'ल स्टे इनसाइड। आई'ल रीड माई बुक।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मुझे लगता है कि हम घर के अंदर रहेंगे। मैं अपनी किताब पढ़ूँगा।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: That sounds boring. Will you play a game with me?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: दैट साउंड्स बोरिंग। विल यू प्ले अ गेम विथ मी?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: यह उबाऊ लगता है। क्या तुम मेरे साथ कोई खेल खेलोगे?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, I'll play a game with you. We'll have fun.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, आई'ल प्ले अ गेम विथ यू। वी'ल हैव फन।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैं तुम्हारे साथ खेल खेलूँगा। हम मज़ा करेंगे।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Great! I'm going to make hot tea when it rains.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ग्रेट! आई'म गोइंग टू मेक हॉट टी वेन इट रेंस।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: बहुत बढ़िया! जब बारिश होगी तो मैं गर्म चाय बनाने वाली हूँ।

audioTranscriptFull:
Radio: Good evening! Here is the weather for tomorrow. In the morning, it'll be very sunny and hot. But in the afternoon, it'll rain heavily. It will be cold.
Wife: Did you hear that, Amit? It'll rain tomorrow afternoon.
Amit: Yes. That is bad news. We'll have to change our plans.
Wife: What will we do? We can't go to the park.
Amit: I know. In the morning, I'll wash the car because it'll be sunny.
Wife: Okay. And what about the afternoon?
Amit: I think we'll stay inside. I'll read my book.
Wife: That sounds boring. Will you play a game with me?
Amit: Yes, I'll play a game with you. We'll have fun.
Wife: Great! I'm going to make hot tea when it rains.

questionSet.questionText: What will the weather be like in the morning tomorrow?
What does Amit say he will do in the morning?
Listen to the radio announcer. Fill in the missing word exactly as he says it: '____ be very sunny.'
Where were Amit and his wife planning to go before they heard the weather?
What will they do in the afternoon instead?
What does the wife want to do in the afternoon? ('Will you _____?').
How does Amit reply when she asks him to play?
options: Yes, I will played a game

questionSet.questionText: What is the wife 'going to' make when it rains?

scoringRules.partialCreditRules: No partial credit for Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, Q7.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You were perfectly able to understand the quick, squished future sounds ('I'll', 'It'll', 'We'll')!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Ensure you playback the audio on Slow mode. Listen specifically for the tiny 'L' sound attached to the words. 'I'll' means 'I will'!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good ear! Watch out for what they said they couldn't do. They couldn't go to the PARK because of the rain.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Radio: Good evening! Here is the weather for tomorrow... (matches full transcript)

hintText: Pay attention to 'It'll'. It sounds almost like 'Little' without the L! 'It'll rain'.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'It'll' is entirely new vocabulary, rather than 'It will'.
